defs: The act or practice of steering, or directing; as, the steerage of a ship.
quotes: He left the city, and, in a most tempestuous season, forsook the helm and steerage of the commonwealth.

defs: The effect of the helm on a ship; the manner in which an individual ship is affected by the helm.
The hinder part of a vessel; the stern.

Properly, the space in the after part of a vessel, under the cabin, but used generally to indicate any part of a vessel having the poorest accommodations and occupied by passengers paying the lowest rate of fare.

quotes: He that hath the steerage of my course.

defs: That by which a course is directed.
quotes: Here he hung on high, The steerage of his wings.
